definitions.text: The same height at all places; parallel to a flat ground.
examples: This table isn't quite level; see how this marble rolls off it?

definitions.text: At the same height as some reference; constructed as level with.
examples: We tried to hang the pictures so that the bottom of the frames were level with the dark line in the wallpaper.
His pulse has been level for 12 hours.

definitions.text: A tool for finding whether a surface is level, or for creating a horizontal or vertical line of reference.
examples: Hand me the level so I can tell if this is correctly installed.

definitions.text: A distance relative to a given reference elevation.
examples: By the end of the day, we'd dug down to the level of the old basement floor.
The sound level is much too high; this hurts my ears.
We've reached a new level of success.

definitions.text: To adjust so as to make as flat or perpendicular to the ground as possible.
examples: You can level the table by turning the pads that screw into the feet.

definitions.text: To destroy by reducing to ground level; to raze.
